title: carum vulgare
intro: Carum vulgare, commonly known as wild caraway or caraway, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in treating digestive issues, such as indigestion and flatulence. The health benefits of caraway include its ability to reduce bloating, alleviate nausea, and support the immune system. Caraway seeds can be used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and capsules, and are also used in cooking, particularly in baked goods and breads, to add flavor and nutritional value.
